title: monitoring — field classification
description: Monitoring carries system-health signals + hashed tenant identifiers. NO customer PII on any monitoring row — the health_check_runs.response_body is scanned + redacted at write time. Provider credentials in monitoring_provider_configs.config are RESTRICTED (financial-critical). Alert + incident context is confidential (may reference internal service names + error messages that leak account structure).

confidential: HealthCheck.target (may contain internal service names, connection strings, URLs — competitor intel; only egressed to admin + ops role)
HealthCheckRun.response_body (post-PII-redaction — but may still contain internal service response structures)
HealthCheckRun.error_details (provider-side error messages that leak account structure)
MonitoringAlert.signal_context (contains check_id, endpoint, source_service — internal architecture references)
MonitoringIncident.description (ops-authored — may reference internal ticket IDs, workaround plans, postmortem summaries)
restricted: HealthCheck.target (ENCRYPTED AT REST — may contain connection strings with embedded credentials, HTTP endpoint URLs with auth tokens, private DNS hostnames)

MonitoringProviderConfig.config (ENCRYPTED AT REST — contains DSNs, API keys, HMAC secrets, OAuth refresh tokens. NEVER decrypted in logs, notifications, broadcasts, or wire projections. Only the driver factory reads decrypted values.)
audit_log: HealthCheck.target (every mutation — encrypted in audit blob)
MonitoringAlertPolicy.suppression_config (every silence — compliance-relevant for the after-hours-suppress trail)
MonitoringProviderConfig.config (every mutation — retention 7 years; encrypted ciphertext in audit blob)

notes: MonitoringProviderConfig.config is RESTRICTED (highest classification) — leaked credentials give an attacker the ability to dispatch fraudulent alerts on the tenant's ad account. Encryption at rest is mandatory; the config field is NEVER included in broadcasts, notifications, or audit blobs (audit blob stores the encrypted ciphertext, not the plaintext).

HealthCheck.target is also RESTRICTED because it may contain embedded credentials in URLs (e.g. 'https://user:pass@host/probe') or connection strings (e.g. 'postgres://user:pass@host/db'). Encrypted at rest with the same infrastructure as provider configs.

HealthCheckRun.response_body is scanned for PII regex patterns (email, phone E.164, SSN, credit card) + redacted with '[REDACTED]' placeholders before persist. Post-redaction, the field is 'confidential' (not restricted) because the redaction removes the PII exposure.
PCI-DSS scope: NO card / PAN / CVV fields on any monitoring row. This is an ops-signal store, not a payment store.
GDPR Art. 17 (right to erasure): monitoring rows carry NO customer PII beyond tenant_id. TenantErased FK CASCADE handles the full erasure; no partial redaction path needed.
